title: RenamePlan
description: The stable, surface-neutral result of a codegraph rename. Emitted byte-identically by the CLI `-j, --json` flag (FR-027) and the codegraph_rename MCP tool result (SC-005). Field names are identical across both surfaces. All offsets are UTF-16 code units (SPEC-008 pin). Byte-identical parity requires a canonical serialization: stable object-key order, UTF-8, no insignificant whitespace, and the deterministic array ordering noted on `edits`. The MCP tool returns this JSON as its text result content (matching the repo's textResult convention in src/mcp/tools.ts), not structuredContent. One envelope carries three result shapes; a consumer branches on `applied` and `refusal`: (1) a successful PLAN (dry-run, or an apply that reached post-check-green) carries `target`, `edits` (>=1, incl. the declaration edit), `confidence`, and per-edit `source`, with `applied` false (dry-run) or true (apply-green); (2) a recoverable REFUSAL carries `refusal` (success-shaped, `applied` false) and MAY omit `target`/`edits`/`confidence`, since a refused request (ambiguous-target, not-indexed, target-not-found, invalid-argument, ...) has no resolved target or edit set; (3) an APPLY terminal additionally carries `outcome`, with `danglingReferences` on `rolled-back` and `recovery` on `rollback-failed`. Only `newName` (the echoed request) and `applied` are present in every shape, so they are the sole always-required fields.

target.description: The resolved target symbol's identity (name + kind + declaration location). Not a bare string — it is the disambiguated node the plan renames.

name.description: The symbol's current name (the old name).

kind.description: NodeKind of the target (e.g. function, method, class, interface, type_alias, enum, constant).

edits.description: Ordered set of edits the plan would make, sorted deterministically by (file path, then range start line, then start character) so the CLI --json and MCP results are byte-identical (SC-005/FR-027). An empty-references plan still contains the declaration edit (never an empty array).

confidence.description: Aggregate confidence over all edits. Drives the --apply gate (FR-015): contains-heuristic refuses apply unless --include-heuristic / includeHeuristic:true.

source.description: Which derivation path produced the plan. Present when the whole plan came from one path; per-edit `source` is authoritative.

lspDegradation.description: Present when an ok-status LSP workspace edit was rejected as unusable and the WHOLE rename degraded to the graph derivation instead (never a per-file merge): `incomplete-coverage` (SPEC-010 D3) — it was missing at least one file the graph already knows carries a span-verified occurrence of the target; `overlapping-edits` (SPEC-010 D5c) — two of its own edit ranges genuinely overlap within a file (spec.md's overlapping-range clause); `unsupported-edits` (SPEC-010 A2) — it carried an edit shape the symbol writer cannot honor (a documentChanges resource operation, or an in-root edit with a multiline range or empty live-derived oldText). Absent whenever `source` is `lsp`, and absent for the FR-003a unavailable/runtime-failed degradation routes (those carry no reason here).

leftoverMentions.description: Optional, non-gating FYI count of old-name occurrences NOT edited (comments, docstrings, strings, and dispatch sites of synthesized heuristic edges). FR-013. Omitted when not computed.

applied.description: Whether side effects occurred. false for every dry-run (FR-001). true only on an --apply / apply:true call that wrote and passed the post-check.

outcome.description: Apply outcome (Slice 2). Absent/ignored on a dry-run. `applied` = post-check green; `refused` = a recoverable gate refusal with zero writes; `rolled-back` = wrote then restored byte-identically; `rollback-failed` = the sole malfunction (FR-019a).

danglingReferences.description: Present on outcome `rolled-back` (FR-019): the old-name references the post-check found still dangling after the write, which forced the unconditional rollback. Machine-actionable — the caller sees exactly what blocked the rename without reading a file.

file.description: Workspace-relative path of the file still carrying the old name.

resyncFailed.description: Present (true) on a `rolled-back` outcome whose own post-restore re-sync failed (threw or returned the lock-failure zero-shape) after the bytes were restored (SPEC-010 B2 review finding). The workspace is restored but the index is stale — the caller must run `codegraph sync`.

character.description: 0-based offset in UTF-16 code units on that line (SPEC-008).

file.description: Workspace-relative path of the edited file.

oldText.description: The exact span text being replaced (the old name occurrence). Verified against live bytes.

newText.description: The replacement text (the new name occurrence).

lineText.description: The edited span's full source line BEFORE the edit (verbatim, no trailing newline). Lets a JSON/MCP consumer render the before/after preview without opening the file (SC-001); the engine already holds these bytes from the plan-time span read (FR-005). When multiple edits share a `file` and `range` start line, each carries an identical lineText; the after-line for that source line is composed by applying every such edit to a single copy, ordered right-to-left by `range` start character, rather than treating each edit's lineText as an independent before/after pair (FR-027).

confidence.description: Per-edit tier from the FR-004 table. Every edit — regardless of tier — also passed span verification (FR-005/FR-016).

refusal.description: Present on a recoverable refusal (FR-007/FR-010/FR-011/FR-015/FR-016/FR-017/not-indexed). Success-shaped on both surfaces — never isError (FR-023). The one exception (rollback-failed, FR-019a) is error-shaped and carries `recovery` instead.

message.description: Human-actionable guidance that names the exact fix/retry.

candidates.description: For ambiguous-target: every matching symbol with the qualifier that uniquely selects it (FR-007).

selector.description: The exact qualifier (e.g. `Class.method`, `--file X`, `--kind K`) that selects this candidate.

files.description: For out-of-root / scope-ignored (FR-017): the offending file path(s) that caused the whole-plan refusal. Also carries the drifted file(s) for a `stale-span` refusal (FR-016), so the caller can act (re-sync) without a file read.

gatedEdits.description: For heuristic-gated: the below-exact edits blocking apply (FR-015).

validKinds.description: For invalid-argument on an unrecognized `kind` value (FR-021a): every recognized NodeKind, so the caller can retry with a valid value without a file read.

recovery.description: Present ONLY on outcome `rollback-failed` (FR-019a) — the sole error-shaped malfunction (MCP isError:true / CLI exit 4). Reports the post-rollback restore state so a caller can retry the restore step alone (an idempotent write-back of known snapshot bytes), never the rename itself.

restoredFiles.description: Touched files confirmed restored byte-identically from snapshot.

unrestoredFiles.description: Touched files whose snapshot could NOT be written back (EACCES / ENOSPC / EPERM / ...).

recoveryDir.description: Per-incident directory holding the unrestored snapshots: .codegraph/rename-recovery-<pid>-<hex>/. Absent (SPEC-010 B5) when the recovery dump itself also failed — the unrestored files still need manual attention, but no dump was written.

writeFailure.description: Present when a Rung-4 write/rename malfunction (EACCES / ENOSPC / ...) forced the rollback that led to outcome `rolled-back` or `rollback-failed` (review finding). Orthogonal to which of those two outcomes this accompanies — absent when the rollback was instead forced by a post-check dangle or a sync lock-failure, which are self-evident from `danglingReferences`.

file.description: Workspace-relative path of the file whose write/rename threw.
